title: scribing-debt fail-close at the close boundary + loud logged waiver (D2, D4)
action: In handleStateSet (bee.mjs), when the incoming phase is 'compounding-complete', call scribingDebt(root) from cells.mjs (bee.mjs may import it — it already imports both libs; state.mjs may NOT) and REFUSE when count > 0. The refusal must NAME every unscribed cell id and point at bee-scribing. Add a `--waive-scribing-debt` flag (D4): it permits the close, but is never silent — it logs a decision recording the waived cell ids, and the refusal text mentions it exists. Register the flag in command-registry.mjs for state.set. RED-first tests: compounding -> compounding-complete with debt>0 is REFUSED and the message contains each debt cell id; the same with --waive-scribing-debt passes AND appends a decision naming the waived cells; with debt==0 it passes with no decision written. Keep both trees byte-identical.

truths: compounding -> compounding-complete with capped unscribed behavior_change cells is refused
the refusal names every unscribed cell id, not just a count
--waive-scribing-debt permits the close and logs a decision naming the waived cells
a close with zero debt passes and writes no waiver decision
scribingDebt is imported in bee.mjs only — state.mjs stays cells-free (no cycle)

trace.outcome: Scribing debt is now a WALL at the close boundary and a signal everywhere else. closeGuardScribingDebt() in bee.mjs (NOT state.mjs — cells.mjs already imports state.mjs, so a back-import would close the cycle state.mjs:6-7 exists to avoid) refuses --phase compounding-complete while capped behavior_change cells are unscribed, naming every one. --waive-scribing-debt is the sanctioned door: it permits the close but logs a decision naming every waived cell. A refused close leaves the phase untouched.
trace.verification_evidence:
=== The post-mortem's exact end state, now refused ===
Fixture: phase=compounding, feature=demo, two capped behavior_change cells (d-1, d-2), last_scribing_run absent.

$ bee state set --phase compounding-complete
set: refusing to close this feature — 2 capped behavior_change cell(s) have not been synced to docs/specs/: d-1, d-2.
"compounding-complete" asserts that scribing already ran for them. It has not.
FIX: run bee-scribing to merge the settled behavior into its area spec, then `bee state scribing-run ...` to stamp it.
If the behavior genuinely belongs in no spec, close with --waive-scribing-debt — it is permitted, but it logs a decision naming every cell you waived.
  → exit 1, state.json phase still "compounding" (no partial write)

$ bee state set --phase compounding-complete --waive-scribing-debt
  → exit 0, phase written, and .bee/decisions.jsonl gains a decision naming d-1 and d-2

=== test suite ===
test_lib: 292 passed, 0 failed
test_bee_cli: 121 passed, 0 failed

New tests, all passing:
- close REFUSED while debt stands; refusal names every unscribed cell id; discloses the waiver door; no partial write
- --waive-scribing-debt permits the close AND logs a decision naming the waived cells
- a close with ZERO debt passes and writes NO waiver decision (nothing was waived)
